These versions of Bison could use more specific options, like `-r itemset,lookahead,solved`, but I haven't tried that on older versions.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2486429212/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426","id":2676159522,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6fgvQi","number":5426,"title":"Syntax vs. Semantic error","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-20T14:56:12Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T14:56:12Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"There are a set of error tests that look something like:
```f90
program kind2
implicit none

real(*) a

end program
```
which currently return
```
semantic error: Expected initialization expression for kind
 --> tests/errors/kind2.f90:4:6
  |
4 | real(*) a
  |      ^ 
```
I'm working on tightening up the parser in places, and, according to the standard, this statement is _syntactically_ invalid, not semantically.  With rules like the standard's _kind-selector_ in place, I get a parsing error of:
```
syntax error: Token ')' is unexpected here
 --> tests/errors/kind2.f90:4:7
  |
4 | real(*) a
  |       ^ 
```
since `*` is not a valid _scalar-int-constant-expr_.  How do people feel about this sort of change?  I'm tagging @gxyd as they put in the `kind_star_of_int` test that first pointed out this change.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

I'm tagging @gxyd as they put in the `kind_star_of_int` test that first caught this change.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2489388484","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426#issuecomment-2489388484","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5426","id":2489388484,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6UYQ3E","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-20T19:30:12Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T19:30:12Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"I appreciate the insight, and completely agree that the current error message is more rich.  I'm not hung-up on the "semantic" vs "syntax" error label, although the latter is probably more accurate.  

This issue came up as I'm working on #5352: handling (valid) syntax like 
```f90
implicit integer(kind=4)(a)
implicit real(4+4)(q-s)
```
causes more shift/reduce errors in the parser, so I was trying to see if more specific parsing rules for _kind-selector_ would reduce the number of errors.  I don't think that it does, so I can back those changes out.

At some point though, we can't diagnose all syntax errors without essentially accepting all input as valid.  Using a modification of your example, `real(,) a` would result in the same syntax error shown in my original comment.  Does `real(*) a` "deserve" better treatment?  Maybe, if we have data that it is a common error.   But adding more ambiguity to the parser may mean a larger and slower parser, so we'll have to keep an eye on that.
  ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2489388484/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

```sh
cmake \
    -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug \
    -DWITH_LLVM=yes \
    -DWITH_STACKTRACE=yes \
    -DWITH_LLVM_STACKTRACE=yes \
    -DWITH_RUNTIME_STACKTRACE=yes \
    -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=`pwd`/inst \
    -DWITH_LSP=no \
    -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="$CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH_LFORTRAN;$CONDA_PREFIX" \
    -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=`pwd`/inst \
    -DCMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR=share/lfortran/lib \
    -GNinja \
    .
```
I think it was the `WITH_LLVM_STACKTRACE` that did the trick.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2495538062/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5464","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5464/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5464/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5464/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5464","id":2686279170,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6gHV4C","number":5464,"title":"Parser requires kind on data-implied-do","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-23T17:05:39Z","updated_at":"2024-11-23T17:05:39Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Attempting to compile:
```f90
program foo
  integer, parameter :: n = 4
  real, dimension(n) :: arr
  data (arr(i), integer :: i = 1,n) /0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0/
  print *, arr
end program foo
```
leads to 
```
syntax error: Token '=' is unexpected here
 --> data.f90:4:30
  |
4 |   data (arr(i), integer :: i = 1,n) /0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0/
  |                              ^ 
```

PR separates the rules _declaration-type-spec_, _intrinsic-type-spec_, and _integer-type-spec_ into their own Bison rules for consistent  use in other parts of the grammar.  For example `integer_type_spec` is now used in the implied-do `data_object`, and `declaration_type_spec` can be used in IMPLICIT statements.  This does _not_ affect the generalized parsing of KIND attributes.

This closes #5464.
This close #5426.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5466/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5466/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2495610506","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5466#issuecomment-2495610506","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5466","id":2495610506,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Uv_6K","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-23T18:36:57Z","updated_at":"2024-11-23T18:36:57Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Okay, I must be missing some testing step. ```f90
subroutine foo
  implicit none ()
end subroutine foo
```
with `lfortran --show-ast` erroneously results in:
```
syntax error: Token ')' is unexpected here
 --> implicit.f90:2:18
  |
2 |   implicit none ()
  |                  ^ 
```
R866 of the F23 draft gives the grammar as `IMPLICIT NONE [ ( [` _implicit-none-spec-list_  `] ) ]`, so an empty pair of parens should be accepted, and treated the same as if no parens were given (or `IMPLICIT NONE (TYPE)`).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5522/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5522/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","number":5523,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5523","id":2207992842,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6Dm0wK","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5523","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5523.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5523.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5523","number":5523,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Allow parser to accept `IMPLICIT NONE ()`","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Simple parser change to allow `IMPLICIT NONE ()` to be accepted.  AST->ASR already handles this case.

* Rename `implicit_none_spec_list` to `implicit_none_spec_star`
* Add `%empty` branch to `implicit_none_spec_star`

{"action":"opened","number":5536,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/pulls/5536","id":2208615044,"node_id":"PR_kwDOCpMuHc6DpMqE","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5536","diff_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5536.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/pull/5536.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5536","number":5536,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Support _implicit-spec-list_ in `IMPLICIT` statements","user":{"login":"pjh40","id":40867327,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQwODY3MzI3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/40867327?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40","html_url":"https://github.com/pjh40","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/pjh40/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Working with F77 validation suite FCVS_95 (see #480 ) demonstrated that our support for `IMPLICIT` statements was incomplete, particularly in that we only allow one _implicit-spec_ per _implicit-stmt_, instead of an _implicit_spec_list_.  An example statement that is now properly accepted is be:
```f90
implicit real(i), integer(b)
```

 This PR adds that support, with changes in the AST grammar, the parser, AST symbol table visitor, and AST->src conversion.  AST->ASR conversion already supported this structure.

The PR also updates the test reference AST files, and adds the new test `implicit13`.  After these changes, FCVS_95 tests that use _implicit-spec-list_ no longer fail (on those statements, at least).

This closes #5352 
This closes #3113 ```f90
subroutine foo
  implicit integer(Y, V-X), real(M)
end subroutine foo
```
with LFortran SHA 4068df32e leads to the error:
```
syntax error: Token ',' is unexpected here
 --> func.f90:2:27
  |
2 |   implicit integer(Y, V-X), real(M)
  |                           ^ 
```
I'd guess that the grammar doesn't accept an _implicit-spec-list_, as is specified in the standard.

Also, _kind-selector_ inputs that aren't an `id` or `KW_INTEGER` are improperly rejected:
```f90
implicit real(4+4)(q-s)
